Conditions: From 1 May 2025 and every three months thereafter, the approved provider must submit to the Regulatory Authority its Quality Improvement Plans for any education and care services in NSW that are rated at Working Towards the National Quality Standard.
These plans must demonstrate progress towards addressing all quality areas rated as Working Towards. From 1 May 2025 the approved provider must ensure that, in respect to each of its education and care services in NSW:
a. any allegations or incidents involving physical or sexual abuse of a child in education and care at its services; and
b. any circumstance arising at any of its services that poses a risk to the health, safety or wellbeing of child
are reported to the Regulatory Authority as soon as practicable, but not later than 24 hours, of the matter being reported to an educator, nominated supervisor, or area manager.

Conditions: Prior to applying for service approval or a transfer,  Devyani Arvind Sapre must undertake professional development in Quality Area 1 (Educational Program and practice) of the National Quality Standard and Quality Area 7 (Governance and leadership) of the National Quality Standard.
• The professional development must be delivered by a recognised early childhood organisation or expert, and approved in writing by the Department prior to undertaking the professional development.
• The Department must be provided with proof of completion of the professional development within 7 days of completion.
The person with management or control of the approved provider must attend an educative session with the Western Metropolitan Regional Quality Assessment and Regulation team within 3 months of being granted provider approval. The focus for the educative session is to gain a comprehensive understanding of the statutory obligations of the National Law and Regulations, Child Safe Standard requirements, including Awareness of Child Protection obligations.

Provider Approval Number: PR-00002383
Legal Name: P Illesinghe & M Illesinghe ATF the M & P Illesinghe Family Trust
Address: 19 Tara Close
Suburb: ROWVILLE
State: VIC
Postcode: 3178
Date Approval Granted: 29/12/2011
Conditions: By 31 March 2024, the Approved Provider must:

1. Ensure that the persons with management or control, nominated supervisor, educational leader, and educators employed, appointed, or engaged to work in the service, attend an educative forum conducted by the Regulatory Authority in relation to the content and application of the Education and Care Services National Law Act 2010 and the Education and Care Services National Regulations 2011, including requirements of the National Quality Standard.

2. Review and revise service policies in relation to the staffing code of conduct, interactions with children (including matters set out in regulation 155 and 156), and the child safe environment. Ensuring all staff are aware of their responsibility to prioritise and safeguard children’s safety, including clear and deliberate processes to embed a culture of child safety, wellbeing, and reporting. Also ensure a copy of the revised policies is to be provided to the Regulatory Authority.

3. Ensure that the persons with management or control, nominated supervisor, educational leader, and all educators employed, appointed, or engaged to work for the approved provider, and any education and care service operated by the approved provider, are provided with professional development and training to:
i. Increase individuals’ understanding of the child safe standards, ensuring all staff are aware of their roles and responsibilities to respond to every child at risk of abuse or neglect, and develop a culture of child safety; and
ii. Increase the knowledge and skills of all individuals to enable them to positively interact with children and give each child positive guidance and encouragement towards acceptable behaviour.

4. Ensure the professional development training outlined above is delivered by a recognised early childhood organisation, consultant, or expert.

5. Ensure Written approval is obtained from the Regulatory Authority as to the suitability of the training organisation, consultant, or expert AND the professional development training plan prior to them being engaged.

6. Ensure, within 7 days of completion of the professional development training, documentary evidence is provided to the Regulatory Authority of each individual staff members successful completion of the professional development training.

•		The Department must be provided with proof of completion of the professional development within 7 days of completion

Conditions: 3.  The approved provider must ensure that all educators employed with Yarriambiack Shire Council attend an information forum conducted by the 
Department in relation to the content and application of the Education and Care Services National Law Act 2010 and the Education and Care Services National Regulations 2011 by 15 June 2015. 4.  The approved provider must ensure that all services where children are delivered to and/or collected from the education and care service premises by bus, document these arrangements in the service’s policy and procedures for the delivery of children to and collection of children from the education and care service premises and that the service provides a copy of the service’s policy and procedures for the delivery and collection of children to and collection of children from the education and care service premises to the parents of children being transported by bus to and from the service the 15 June 2015. 5. The approved provider must ensure that all services where children are transported as part of the education and care service have a documented policy and procedure that outlines these arrangements including the requirements for the excursion, the risk assessment for the excursion and for authorisation for the excursion by the 15 June 2015.

Conditions: The approved provider must ensure that a child being educated and cared for by the service is not transported by the service or on transportation arranged by the service until 30 June 2024. The approved provider must engage a consultant until 30 June 2024. The consultant must specialise in early childhood education and have the ability to support improvements to practices at the service with a focus on the following areas:
a) Regulation 102 B - Transport risk assessment must be conducted before service transports child
b) Regulation 102 C - Conduct of risk assessment for transporting of children by the education and care service
c) Regulation 102 D - Authorisation for service to transport children
d) Regulation 102E - Children embarking a means of transport— centre-based service
e) Regulation 102F Children disembarking a means of transport—centre-based service The approved provider must submit a Quality Improvement Plan or Self- Assessment for the service to the Regulatory Authority by 30 June 2023 and every 3 months thereafter to 30 June 2024. The Quality Improvement Plan must include an assessment by the approved provider of the quality of the practices of the service against the National Quality Standard and any areas that the approved provider considers may require improvement and a statement of the philosophy of the service.

Conditions: 1.	The approved provider must provide evidence to the relevant regulatory authority, upon request, that all nominated supervisors, persons in day-to-day charge, and staff members at each education and care service under its operation have: 
o	been made aware of the service’s policies and procedures 
o	been informed how compliance and application of the policies and procedures will be monitored. 2.	To ensure that the approved provider meets their reporting obligations under the National Law, upon request by the relevant regulatory authority, the approved provider must provide evidence of:
o	an internal reporting system for each education and care service under its operation that enables all nominated supervisors, persons in day-to-day charge, and staff members to promptly escalate to the approved provider,
o	how this system will be monitored and reviewed. 3.	The approved provider must submit a support management plan to the relevant regulatory authority for each education and care service prior to commencing operations. The plan should include but not limited to:
o	support measures in place for all nominated supervisors, persons in day-to-day charge, and staff members during the first twelve (12) months of operation
o	a schedule of visits by the provider’s representatives to oversee and support quality practices, 
o	strategies to ensure ongoing compliance and continuous improvement in service delivery.

Provider Approval Number: PR-40001112
Legal Name: Affinity Education Group Pty Limited
Address: GPO Box 440
Suburb: BRISBANE
State: QLD
Postcode: 4001
Date Approval Granted: 04/10/2013
Conditions: The approved provider must not hold more than the existing 100 service approvals for the provision of education and care services in NSW. This condition applies from 1 October 2025 and will remain in force until such time as, in respect to its service approvals for NSW education and care services:
a. 95% or more of those services are rated at or above Meeting the National Quality Standard (calculated based on the number of services in NSW that are currently rated and, of those services, the percentage that are rated at Meeting the NQS or above (and rounded to the nearest whole percentage). In addition to condition 1, the approved provider agrees that in the event that any of its existing services are divested it will not seek to add any education and care services to its existing portfolio until 1 March 2026, to allow it to place an increased focus on compliance and operational improvement across its existing education and care services operating in NSW. From the date of the commencement of these conditions and every three months thereafter, the approved provider must submit to the Regulatory Authority its Quality Improvement Plans for:
a. each of its NSW education and care services that are rated at Working Towards the National Quality Standard as at 1 October 2025; and 
b. any of its NSW education and care services that are rated at Working Towards the National Quality Standard after 1 October 2025 but before 30 September
2026. This condition excludes any services with an existing rating of Working Towards the National Quality Standards that are acquired by the approved provider after 1 March 2026
These plans must demonstrate progress towards addressing all quality areas rated as Working Towards. From 1 October 2025 the approved provider must ensure that in each of its NSW education and care services that are rated at Working Towards the National Quality Standards as at 1 October 2025, the educator to child ratios are maintained above the requirements of the National Law as per the following required levels:
a. for children from birth to 24 months of age - 1 educator to 3 children;
b. for children over 24 months of age and less than 36 months of age—1 educator to 4 children; and
c. for children aged 36 months of age or over (not including children over preschool age) - 1 educator to 8 children.
This condition remains in force in respect to each service for a period of 15 months or until such time as the service is re-assessed and achieves a rating of Meeting the 
National Quality Standards or above, whichever is the earlier. Within three months of the commencement of these conditions the approved provider must submit to the Regulatory Authority for approval a detailed plan to provide additional child safety training to all staff currently working at each of its NSW education and care services. This plan must include:
a. The details of the training provider(s) that will deliver the sessions
b. The content that will be included
c. The specific staff that the training will be delivered to Upon approval of the detailed plan (see condition 5) the approved provider must provide confirmation that the training has been delivered to all staff currently working at each of its NSW education and care services within six months from the date of approval of the plan Within three months of the commencement of these conditions the approved provider must submit to the Regulatory Authority a detailed plan documenting a program for the installation of CCTV at all of its NSW education and care services. The approved provider must provide quarterly reports to the Regulatory Authority documenting progress on the installation of CCTV at its NSW education and care services.

o	The professional development must be delivered by a recognised early childhood organisation or expert and approved in writing by the Department prior to undertaking the professional development.
o	The Department must be provided with proof of completion of the professional development within 7 days of completion.

Provider Approval Number: PR-00003093
Legal Name: Matthew Hine
Address: PO BOX 443
Suburb: HAMPTON PARK
State: VIC
Postcode: 3976
Date Approval Granted: 29/12/2011
Conditions: The Approved Provider must ensure that from 1 March 2020: 
•	Whenever 15 or less children are being educated and cared for by the service, there is at least:
 
o	One educator who holds at least an approved diploma level education and care qualification; or 
o	Two educators working directly with children; at least one educator who is enrolled and studying an approved diploma level education and care qualification and one educator who is enrolled and working towards at least an approved certificate III level education and care qualification.
 The Approved Provider must:

•	Provide professional development to the person with management or control, the nominated supervisor and persons in day-to-day charge to ensure that they gain knowledge to build their skills in leadership and to ensure at each service, persons in a leadership role understand their roles and responsibilities to operate the service in compliance with the National Law and National Regulations by 31 March 2020; and

•	The professional development must be delivered by a recognised early childhood expert or consultant and be approved in writing by the Department prior to commencement of the training; and 

•	The Department must be provided with proof of the completion of the professional development within 7 days of completion.
 The Approved Provider must review and revise the services staffing policy and procedures to ensure that:

•	the procedures for determining the persons with management or control, nominated supervisor, person in day-to-day charge and the induction of educators, co-ordinators and staff members is comprehensive; and

•	that educators and staff that commence at the service have an understanding of their roles and their responsibilities; and 

•	A copy of the reviewed and revised policies and procedures are provided to the Department by 31 March 2020.
